Documentation for pymc.math.concatenate refers to `join` which is not documented.","user":{"login":"DrEntropy","id":6955761,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjY5NTU3NjE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6955761?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/DrEntropy","html_url":"https://github.com/DrEntropy","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DrEntropy/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DrEntropy/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DrEntropy/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DrEntropy/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DrEntropy/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DrEntropy/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DrEntropy/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DrEntropy/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DrEntropy/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":4823685116,"node_id":"LA_kwDOIXTtyc8AAAABH4OX_A","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/pymc-devs/pytensor/labels/docs","name":"docs","color":"5319e7","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-02-16T17:58:37Z","updated_at":"2024-02-16T17:58:37Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Issue with current documentation:

https://www.pymc.io/projects/docs/en/stable/api/generated/pymc.math.concatenate.html  which is based on the docstring for pytensor.math.concatenate says : 
```
Alias for `join`(axis, *tensor_list).

This function is similar to `join`, but uses the signature of
numpy's concatenate function.
```

This is not very helpful, because `join` is not documented as far as I can determine.

### Idea or request for content:

I suggest instead that the docstring  be based on the  documentation at 
https://pytensor.readthedocs.io/en/latest/library/tensor/basic.html#pytensor.tensor.concatenate

","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/pymc-devs/pytensor/issues/648/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/pymc-devs/pytensor/issues/648/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

change adds or pertains to documentation"},{"id":208968668,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyMDg5Njg2Njg=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/good%20first%20issue","name":"good first issue","color":"0052cc","default":true,"description":"Indicates a good issue for first-time contributors to Julia"},{"id":2317339600,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyMzE3MzM5NjAw","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/markdown","name":"markdown","color":"ffa81e","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2023-09-03T20:58:16Z","updated_at":"2023-09-04T15:43:03Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"[Markdown] (https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/master/stdlib/Markdown/docs/src/index.md) documentation should have a section on the existence and use of the `md"##Markdown Here"`  string macro.  As a newbie I ran into this syntax and could not figure out what it was! I suppose it is obvious what it does from experimentation, but I could not find any documentation.   

PS: I ran into it when I was toying around with Pluto.js  where this macro is used extensively.

But it is syntactic sugar for a macro call:  `md"Blah"`  turns into `@md_str("blah")`.
